compressed_regions.summary: Multiple bulk-related backward and forward features exhibit extremely low cardinality with unique values=1 and cardinality ratios near zero, indicating near-constant values across the partition.
supporting_patterns: near-zero variance in bulk and flag features
uncertainty_notes: Low cardinality may reflect measurement saturation or feature design
Uniformity could mask subtle class-dependent variations

compressed_regions.summary: Several feature pairs exhibit perfect or near-perfect correlation (correlation=1 or 0.9993), indicating strong linear dependencies and potential redundancy.
supporting_patterns: redundancy in packet length and flag count features
uncertainty_notes: Perfect correlations may reflect feature engineering or measurement overlap
Dependencies may affect model stability or interpretability

compressed_regions.summary: Several packet length and size features show strong class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and differing unique value counts between BENIGN and DDoS classes.

notes: Unique values differ significantly by class

uncertainty_notes: Variance ratio magnitude varies widely across features
Unique value counts are much higher in BENIGN class, indicating skew

compressed_regions.summary: Partition-level class imbalance ratio is moderate (1.577), and the Destination Port feature shows strong distributional divergence between BENIGN and DDoS classes with high JS divergence and entropy differences.

notes: Partition-level class imbalance and distribution metrics

supporting_patterns: high JS divergence in Destination Port distribution
uncertainty_notes: Class imbalance ratio is moderate, not extreme
Distributional metrics suggest strong class separation on Destination Port but do not confirm predictive validity

compressed_regions.summary: The partition contains a substantial number of duplicated rows (729), indicating potential data redundancy or repeated patterns.
uncertainty_notes: Duplicated rows may affect statistical independence assumptions
Impact on downstream analysis uncertain without further context

preserved_weak_signals.descriptor: Destination Port shows high entropy in BENIGN class but zero entropy in DDoS class, indicating a strong class-dependent distributional difference.
preserved_weak_signals.preservation_reason: Preserves subtle distributional contrast that may be overshadowed by dominant class imbalance or other features.
uncertainty_notes: Entropy difference may reflect protocol or attack behavior but requires further verification

unresolved_tensions.description: Features with near-constant values (low cardinality) coexist with features showing strong class-conditioned structural variation, creating tension between uniformity and class-dependent diversity.
context_notes: Partition exhibits both extreme uniformity in some features and strong class-dependent variation in others
This tension complicates interpretation of feature informativeness
unresolved_tensions.reason_unresolved: Insufficient evidence to determine if low cardinality features mask subtle class signals or represent saturation artifacts.

unresolved_tensions.description: Strong perfect correlations among many feature pairs contrast with the presence of features showing class-conditioned variance and unique value differences, indicating tension between redundancy and diversity.
context_notes: Perfect correlations suggest redundancy, while variance ratios suggest meaningful class variation
This tension may affect feature selection and model stability
unresolved_tensions.reason_unresolved: No direct evidence to resolve whether dependencies obscure or complement class-conditioned variation.
